goal: Record distributed P1 baseline for Gemma 4 31B across Mac (Metal) + PC (CUDA/WSL2) EXO cluster at 8K/16K/32K contexts.
outcome: INFEASIBLE with EXO 1.0 + MLX 0.31.1 on mixed Apple Metal + Linux WSL2/CUDA. Baseline numbers not recorded.
headline_finding: MLX's tensor-parallel ring backend does not exercise CUDA on Linux peers — it silently falls back to CPU. EXO's MlxRing instance type depends on this backend, so Gemma 4 31B (which requires Tensor sharding per EXO's Gemma 4 guard) cannot run distributed across Mac+Metal + PC+CUDA today. The PC's RTX 5090 sat at 8% utilization with the runner pegged at 363% CPU while loading and during the first forward pass, confirming the compute path never reached CUDA.

environment.interconnect: WiFi 192.168.4.0/22, ports 52415 (libp2p) + 49153-65535 (MlxRing ephemeral), opened via Windows firewall rule
environment.mlx_version_known_broken: MLX 0.31.1 per dev.to independent repro (see sources below)

what_we_got_working: Bidirectional libp2p topology (after Windows firewall rule for ephemeral 49153-65535 TCP inbound)
MlxRing tensor-parallel ring init (`Rank 0/1 mlx distributed initialization complete`)
Model download coordination (18.4 GB split between nodes, files pre-cached on disk both sides)
LoadModel completion (11.87s on Mac Metal once files cached; several minutes on PC CPU-bound even though CUDA was meant to be active)

Skip-warmup patch (src/exo/worker/engines/mlx/generator/generate.py): bypasses the JIT-compile cascade in warmup_inference() when EXO_SKIP_WARMUP=1 is set consistently on both ranks, while still participating in the post-warmup all_gather. Patch exercised cleanly (log: 'skipping in-process warmup (EXO_SKIP_WARMUP=1)').

what_stayed_broken.symptom_1_ring_tensor_parallel_hangs: After firewall fix + patch + pre-cached files, LoadModel completed but the first TextGeneration prefill pass ran for 4+ min without emitting a single decode token. Mac's EXO heartbeat eventually timed out PC (5-min inactivity) and issued 'Manually removing node ... due to inactivity', triggering Shutdown cascade and ring abort with 'Too many send/recv errors. Aborting...' (errno 0 recv, errno 32 send).
what_stayed_broken.symptom_2_CUDA_not_used: PC Claude confirmed during the stall: RTX 5090 at 8% utilization, 65W draw. Runner at 363% CPU. VRAM held only stale allocations from previous killed runners (12.7 GB zombie). Standalone single-process MLX-CUDA on the same PC loads the same model in 6.7s and runs forward passes at 7.5 tok/s — MLX itself is healthy. The bug is specific to EXO's MlxRing tensor-parallel distributed path on non-Metal backends.
what_stayed_broken.root_cause_upstream: Independent repro on dev.to: 'mx.distributed.init(backend="ring") hangs indefinitely on the CUDA backend ... The MLX CUDA ring implementation simply doesn't work yet (as of MLX 0.31.1).' Control-plane init reports success, but tensor collectives never actually run on CUDA.

attempted_workarounds.result: EXO rejects: 'Pipeline parallelism is not supported for Gemma 4; use tensor parallelism instead.'
Started 45.6 GB download, cancelled by user decision.

attempted_workarounds.what: EXO_SKIP_WARMUP=1 patch to bypass warmup's JIT compile cascade
attempted_workarounds.result: Patch fires correctly; warmup bypasses; but the first real inference prefill hits the same CPU-bound stall because the underlying MLX-CUDA ring still isn't running on CUDA.

attempted_workarounds.what: Windows firewall rule + WSL2 mirrored/NAT check + portproxy
attempted_workarounds.result: Fixed libp2p topology — ring init works. Doesn't help the compute-path problem.

attempted_workarounds.what: Fresh EXO restarts on both nodes after each ring abort
attempted_workarounds.result: Restores topology. Doesn't change outcome once placement is re-issued.

measurements_captured.ring_ephemeral_port_observed: 49153-65535 range, actual picks were 65169 then 51724 then 51143

options_for_the_matrix.A_wait_for_upstream: MLX fixes the CUDA ring backend; retry with MLX >0.31.1 or whatever version includes the fix. EXO 1.0 demo (Alex Cheema, DGX Spark + M3 Ultra, 4x throughput) likely uses a newer or patched MLX build + disaggregated prefill/decode instead of MlxRing tensor parallel.
options_for_the_matrix.B_disaggregated_prefill_decode_mode: EXO Labs' working cross-arch demos use disaggregated inference (prefill on CUDA node, decode on Metal node, KV-cache streaming). Our EXO build's InstanceMeta enum exposes only MlxRing and MlxJaccl — neither is explicitly the disaggregated mode. Either this mode is auto-selected when topology profiling sees mixed backends (not observed in our logs), or it requires a newer EXO build.
options_for_the_matrix.C_llama_cpp_rpc: Independent repro pivoted here; it works. Mac runs llama.cpp with Metal, offloads specific layers to `llama-server --rpc` on PC CUDA. We already have both builds compiled (vendor/llama.cpp Metal + PC's tqp-v0.1.0 CUDA build). Would give a real distributed baseline for the C1 row, albeit under a different runtime than EXO/MLX.
options_for_the_matrix.D_drop_T_0617_from_the_matrix: C1 row left empty; P1 policy marked blocked-upstream; AutoPilot routes P0/P2-P6 only. Honest and doesn't spend more cycles on a known upstream bug.

recommendation: Path D for now to unblock Phase 6; revisit as Path B or A when MLX / EXO land the fix or when a disaggregated instance type is exposed. Path C (llama.cpp RPC) is a fallback we can spec separately if the matrix really needs a cross-arch distributed cell.
files_changed_this_session: /Users/murai-labs/crossfire/exo/src/exo/worker/engines/mlx/generator/generate.py — skip-warmup patch applied (NOT upstreamed, local-only). Same patch applied on PC.
Windows firewall rule 'EXO MlxRing ephemeral 49153-65535' (TCP, LAN-scoped to 192.168.4.0/22) — added by user on PC host.
